data.title: Microservices-Based
data.description: Amazon.com uses a microservices architecture to manage its vast array of services and features individually.
data.technical_spec: Each service operates independently, allowing for granular updates and scalability.

data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Utilizes distributed systems to handle enormous amounts of data and user requests globally.
data.technical_spec: Data is stored and managed across multiple, geographically dispersed data centers.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Employs load balancing techniques to efficiently distribute incoming network traffic across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Uses AWS Elastic Load Balancing for optimal resource use, high availability, and fault tolerance.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon.com is primarily hosted on AWS, providing scalable cloud infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates services like S3, EC2, and RDS for various operational needs.

data.title: Continuous Integration and Deployment
data.description: Amazon employs CI/CD for rapid deployment and integration of new features.
data.technical_spec: Uses automated pipelines for testing and deploying updates across the platform.
